meaning: n.科威特人adj.科威特的,科威特人的
examples: Patriotic songs have long been a feature of Kuwaiti life.
Were I a Kuwaiti woman wielding a ballot, I would have been a clearer and more present danger.
Kuwaiti prisoners have their ears nailed to a plank of wood.
Al-Kuwaiti eventually lead the CIA to Bin Laden, and, after some deliberation, the raid was authorized.
In 2003 U.S. and Kuwaiti forces intercepted all nine Iraqi-launched missiles with PAC-2 and PAC-3 systems.
